factors.summary: Environmental regulations are a compliance burden, but the company is ahead of requirements.

evidence.text: Any such violations of law or improper actions could: subject us to civil or criminal investigations; lead to substantial civil or criminal, monetary and non-monetary penalties and related shareholder lawsuits; lead to increased costs of compliance; and damage our reputation, our consolidated results of operations, financial condition and cash flows.
evidence.subsection: • Our reputation, ability to do business and results of operations may be impaired by improper conduct by any of our employees, agents, or business partners.

factors.summary: Free cash flow experienced a decrease in 2024 compared to 2023, primarily due to higher tax payments, though there are signs of improvement in cash flow generation which supports operations and growth.

evidence.text: Free cash flow for the year ended December 31, 2024 decreased by $115.8 million compared to 2023 primarily due to tax payments made for the gain on sale of De-Sta-Co and a minority owned equity method investment totaling $123.8 million, partially offset by lower capital expenditures.

We generate substantial cash from the operations of our businesses and remain in a strong financial position, with sufficient liquidity available for upcoming debt maturities and for reinvestment in existing businesses and strategic acquisitions.

Earnings from continuing operations increased primarily from gain on dispositions, pricing actions and benefits from productivity initiatives, partially offset by lower volumes across some of the Company's businesses, and increased selling, general and administrative expenses.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure (CapEx) outlook appears mixed. While capital expenditures decreased in 2024 and 2023, there is a projected increase in 2025, suggesting confidence in future growth. However, the introduction section does not provide significant additional information on CapEx trends.

evidence.text: Our capital expenditures decreased $15.9 million and $27.7 million in 2024 and 2023, respectively, in line with our planned expenditures for the years. We estimate capital expenditures in 2025 to range from $170.0 million to $190.0 million.

Overall bookings for the year ended December 31, 2024 increased 11.1% compared to the prior year, driven by organic growth of 5.8% and acquisition-related growth of 5.6%, partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 0.3%. The organic bookings growth was primarily driven by increased demand for North America above-ground retail fueling equipment and favorable demand trends across our clean energy platform. Segment book-to-bill was 1.00.

factors.summary: Debt leverage has shown improvement with a significant decrease in net debt, as evidenced by the reduction in the net debt to net capitalization ratio. However, other sections indicate stability in debt leverage with manageable interest costs and no significant changes mentioned.

evidence.text: Net debt decreased $2.0 billion during the period primarily due to increased cash and cash equivalents from the disposition of the ESG business. Our net debt to net capitalization ratio decreased to 13.5% at December 31, 2024 compared to 37.3% at December 31, 2023.

evidence.subsection: Reconciliation of Segment Earnings to Earnings from Continuing Operations

evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

factors.summary: Shareholder returns have been maintained through consistent dividend payments, with no share repurchases in 2023. However, the company has utilized share buyback programs in prior years, such as the 2024 ASR Program, indicating a mixed approach to returning value to shareholders.

evidence.text: During the year ended December 31, 2023, the Company repurchased no shares. Total dividend payments to common shareholders were $284.3 million in 2023 and $287.6 million in 2022.
The Company will continue to make proactive adjustments to its cost structure to align with current demand trends.

During the year ended December 31, 2024, the Company received a total of 2,869,282 shares upon completion of a $500 million accelerated repurchase program (the 'ASR Program'). The total number of shares ultimately repurchased under the ASR Program was based on the volume-weighted average share price of Dover's common stock during the calculation period of the accelerated share repurchase program, less a discount, which was $174.26 over the term of the ASR Program. During the year ended December 31, 2024, exclusive of the ASR Program, there were no share repurchases.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation and legal risks, including potential liabilities from product-related injuries, patent disputes, employment claims, and commercial disputes. Additionally, it is exposed to legal and regulatory changes, such as tax audits and indemnification provisions from acquisitions, which could impact its financial health and reputation.

evidence.text: We and certain of our subsidiaries are, and from time to time may become, parties to a number of legal proceedings incidental to our businesses, including alleged injuries arising out of the use of products or exposure to hazardous substances, or claims related to patent infringement, employment matters and commercial disputes.
evidence.subsection: • Unforeseen developments in contingencies such as litigation and product recalls could adversely affect our consolidated results of operations, financial condition and cash flows.

evidence.text: Our businesses are subject to regulation and their profitability and reputation could be adversely affected by domestic and foreign governmental and public policy changes... Any of these factors could adversely affect customer demand, our relationships with customers and suppliers, and our business and financial position.

factors.summary: Overall, the impact of interest rate changes is mixed. While the company's long-term, fixed-rate debt minimizes operational and financial impacts from fluctuating rates, other areas such as pension obligations and customer inventory levels are affected. Higher interest rates have increased borrowing costs and influenced customer behavior, though the overall impact remains manageable.

evidence.text: We consider our current risk related to market fluctuations in interest rates to be minimal since our debt is largely long-term and fixed rate in nature.
The 2024 weighted-average discount rate used to measure our pension benefit obligations for non-US plans decreased to 2.64% from 2.80% in 2023 due to a decrease in interest rates.

The organic revenue decline was primarily due to reduced year-over-year demand in above ground retail fueling equipment, due to the expected roll-off of EMV-related demand in the first half of the year, as well as general reduction in our customers' inventory across our distribution channels, as higher interest rates increased its carrying costs.

For the year ended December 31, 2024, interest expense, net of interest income, decreased $23.8 million, or 20.2%, to $94.0 million compared with 2023 primarily due to interest income generated by the proceeds from the sale of ESG held in highly liquid short-term investments.

factors.summary: The company faces potential challenges with labor costs due to possible increases and turnover, which could negatively impact their operations. However, they have implemented restructuring and cost reduction programs, including headcount reductions and other measures, to manage these costs effectively.

evidence.text: A sustained labor shortage or increased turnover rates within our employee base, increases in the salaries and wages paid by competing employers, as a result of general macroeconomic factors or otherwise, could lead to increased costs, such as increased overtime to meet demand and potentially further increase salaries and wage rates to attract and retain employees, and could negatively affect our ability to efficiently operate our manufacturing facilities and overall business.
evidence.subsection: • Recessions, adverse market conditions or downturns in the markets we serve could adversely affect our operations.

evidence.text: Restructuring and other costs of $85.0 million included restructuring charges of $69.8 million and other costs of $15.2 million. Restructuring and other costs were primarily related to headcount reductions and product line and other exit costs in the Clean Energy & Fueling and Climate & Sustainability Technologies segments. Other costs (benefits) were primarily due to non-cash asset impairment charges and reorganization costs in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies and Imaging & Identification segments, respectively. For further discussion related to our restructuring and other costs, see 'Restructuring and Other Costs (Benefits),' within this Item 7.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements have resulted in translation gains and losses, affecting the net investment in euro-denominated operations.

evidence.text: Changes in the value of the currencies of the countries in which we operate affect our results of operations, financial position and cash flows when translated into U.S. dollars. The strengthening of the U.S. dollar could result in unfavorable translation effects as the results of foreign operations are translated into U.S. dollars. We have generally accepted the exposure to exchange rate movements relative to our investment in non-U.S. operations.
The unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 0.3%. Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue in 2024 by approximately 2.5%.

The increase in revenue was driven by acquisition-related growth of 3.0% primarily in our Clean Energy & Fueling and Pumps & Process Solutions segments, partially offset by a disposition-related decline of 2.0% in our Engineered Products segment and an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 0.2%. Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue in 2024 by approximately 1.6% and by 3.8% in the prior year.

factors.summary: Talent retention is facing challenges due to competition and macroeconomic factors, but the company is managing it through productivity initiatives and cost reduction actions. There is no specific mention of instability in talent retention elsewhere in the filing.

evidence.text: A sustained labor shortage or increased turnover rates within our employee base, increases in the salaries and wages paid by competing employers, as a result of general macroeconomic factors or otherwise, could lead to increased costs, such as increased overtime to meet demand and potentially further increase salaries and wage rates to attract and retain employees, and could negatively affect our ability to efficiently operate our manufacturing facilities and overall business.
evidence.subsection: • Recessions, adverse market conditions or downturns in the markets we serve could adversely affect our operations.

evidence.text: The segment earnings increase was primarily driven by favorable product mix, pricing and productivity initiatives, partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is showing improvement, supported by positive organic growth and increased demand across key business segments. However, there are uncertainties due to geopolitical and economic factors that could impact future performance.

evidence.text: The impacts of any such unexpected event are difficult to predict but could have a material adverse effect on our businesses, financial condition, or operations.
evidence.subsection: • Our businesses or operations may be adversely affected by natural or human-induced disasters, acts of war, terrorism, international conflicts, and public health crises.

evidence.text: Overall bookings for the year ended December 31, 2024 increased 11.1% compared to the prior year, driven by organic growth of 5.8% and acquisition-related growth of 5.6%, partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 0.3%. The organic bookings growth was primarily driven by increased demand for North America above-ground retail fueling equipment and favorable demand trends across our clean energy platform. Segment book-to-bill was 1.00.

The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by improved production performance and increased demand in our vehicle service business, along with favorable demand trends in our aerospace & defense business. We expect positive organic growth in 2025, driven by favorable demand trends in several of our key end markets, most notably in our aerospace and defense businesses.

factors.summary: The company is currently undergoing headcount restructuring, primarily in response to market conditions. This restructuring is ongoing and involves reductions in certain segments, such as Clean Energy & Fueling and Climate & Sustainability Technologies. While these actions aim to improve operational efficiency, there are associated risks, including potential delays in realizing benefits and uncertainties regarding their impact on future financial performance.

evidence.text: Restructuring charges of $69.8 million were primarily related to headcount reductions and product line and other exit costs in the Clean Energy & Fueling and Climate & Sustainability Technologies segments.
evidence.subsection: • Recessions, adverse market conditions or downturns in the markets we serve could adversely affect our operations.

As these plans and actions can be complex, the anticipated operational improvements, efficiencies and other benefits might be delayed or not realized. We are unable to determine the impact that recessions, adverse market conditions or downturns will have on our financial position, operating results and cash flows in future periods.

factors.summary: The supply chain health is showing mixed signals, with some evidence of improvement and others indicating ongoing risks. While lead times are normalizing and there are no significant disruptions reported, geopolitical and economic instability continues to pose risks, especially given the company's significant international operations.

evidence.text: Approximately 46% and 48% of our revenues for 2024 and 2023, respectively, were derived outside the United States and we expect international sales to continue to represent a significant portion of our revenues given our global growth strategy.
evidence.subsection: • We are subject to risks relating to our existing international operations and expansion into new geographical markets.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different segments. While Clean Energy & Fueling and Imaging & Identification are experiencing positive organic growth, the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment is facing a decline. Overall demand remains stable with solid demand across most end markets, though there are challenges such as reduced customer inventory and higher carrying costs due to higher interest rates.

evidence.text: The organic revenue decline was primarily due to reduced year-over-year demand in above ground retail fueling equipment, due to the expected roll-off of EMV-related demand in the first half of the year, as well as general reduction in our customers' inventory across our distribution channels, as higher interest rates increased its carrying costs. This was partially offset by pricing actions aimed at mitigating material cost inflation and strong demand in our fluid transfer solutions and hydrogen and liquefied natural gas clean energy businesses.
The results were driven by acquisitions, solid demand across most end markets and strategic pricing initiatives.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power, successfully raising prices across most segments which positively impacted revenue. The evidence indicates a favorable impact of approximately 1.6% to 3.8% on revenue in 2024 and the prior year.

evidence.text: Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue in 2024 by approximately 2.5%. factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments such as clean energy and imaging, which is beneficial. This shift has positively impacted the gross profit margin, driven by improvements in mix, pricing, and productivity initiatives, despite challenges like inflationary pressures and lower volumes in some areas.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing initiatives and increased demand for marking and coding consumables, partially offset by softer demand in our digital textile printing business.

Gross profit margin increased 90 basis points to 38.2% as compared to the prior year driven by benefits from mix, pricing, price versus cost dynamics and productivity initiatives, partially offset by inflationary headwinds, acquisition integration costs and lower volumes across some of the Company's businesses.

factors.summary: The order backlog is growing, supported by organic bookings growth across most segments, with favorable book-to-bill ratios. However, there are some exceptions where growth is not consistent.

evidence.text: Order backlog and book-to-bill are growing in most segments, with some exceptions.

Overall bookings for the year ended December 31, 2024 increased 11.1% compared to the prior year, driven by organic growth of 5.8% and acquisition-related growth of 5.6%, partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 0.3%. The organic bookings growth was primarily driven by increased demand for North America above-ground retail fueling equipment and favorable demand trends across our clean energy platform. Segment book-to-bill was 1.00.

Bookings increased 7.3% over the prior year to $7.7 billion for the year ended December 31, 2024. This included organic bookings growth of 6.5% and acquisition-related growth of 3.2%, partially offset by a disposition-related decline of 2.1% and an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 0.3%. Segment book-to-bill was 0.97.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth shows mixed performance across different segments, with acceleration in some areas offset by declines in others.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing actions and growth in clean energy solutions and North American above-ground retail fueling equipment, partially offset by lower volumes in fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions due to temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand.

Organic revenue remained flat due to increases of 8.2%, 2.6%, 2.4%, and 1.4% in our Engineered Products, Clean Energy & Fueling, Imaging & Identification, and Pumps & Process Solutions segments, respectively, offset by the Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment which declined 11.2%. For further information, see 'Segment Results of Operations' within this Item 7.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed across the organization, with some segments experiencing declines while others show growth. While unit volumes are growing in most end markets, driven by real demand, there are also reductions in certain areas due to higher interest rates and the expected roll-off of EMV-related demand.

evidence.text: The organic revenue decline was primarily due to reduced year-over-year demand in above ground retail fueling equipment, due to the expected roll-off of EMV-related demand in the first half of the year, as well as general reduction in our customers' inventory across our distribution channels, as higher interest rates increased its carrying costs.

The increase in revenue was driven by acquisition-related growth of 3.0% primarily in our Clean Energy & Fueling and Pumps & Process Solutions segments, partially offset by a disposition-related decline of 2.0% in our Engineered Products segment and an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 0.2%. Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue in 2024 by approximately 1.6% and by 3.8% in the prior year.

factors.summary: Margins are improving overall, driven by pricing, productivity, and cost reduction initiatives, despite some challenges such as inflationary pressures and lower volumes in certain segments.

evidence.text: Segment margin increased to 18.6% from 18.4% in the prior year.

Gross profit margin increased 90 basis points to 38.2% as compared to the prior year driven by benefits from mix, pricing, price versus cost dynamics and productivity initiatives, partially offset by inflationary headwinds, acquisition integration costs and lower volumes across some of the Company's businesses. Segment margin increased to 19.2% from 17.9% in the prior year.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with Clean Energy & Fueling and Imaging & Identification showing strong growth, while Climate & Sustainability Technologies and other geographic markets are experiencing slower growth or declines.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing actions and growth in clean energy solutions and North American above-ground retail fueling equipment, partially offset by lower volumes in fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions due to temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand.

Organic revenue for the U.S., our largest market, grew 3.8% as compared to the prior year, driven by broad-based growth primarily in our Engineered Products and Clean Energy & Fueling segments. Revenue in Asia and Europe declined 7.1% and 3.1%, respectively, while revenue in Other Americas grew 5.6%. All other geographic markets declined 17.4% organically year over year. The results were driven by acquisitions, solid demand across most end markets and strategic pricing initiatives.

factors.summary: Restructuring and productivity initiatives are effectively reducing costs, as evidenced by measurable savings. While the company has incurred significant restructuring charges, these actions are aimed at achieving long-term cost efficiencies and improving profitability. The focus on headcount reductions and product line exits, along with other cost management measures, indicates a strategic approach to cost optimization. Despite the unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation, overall cost reduction efforts are yielding positive results.

evidence.text: Restructuring and productivity initiatives are delivering measurable savings.

Other costs (benefits) were primarily due to non-cash asset impairment charges and reorganization costs in the Climate & Sustainability Technologies and Imaging & Identification segments, respectively.

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives are demonstrating positive impact, driving gains in both pricing and operational efficiency, and contributing to margin expansion despite some offsetting factors.

evidence.text: The segment earnings increase was primarily driven by favorable product mix, pricing and productivity initiatives, partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation.

Gross profit margin increased 90 basis points to 38.2% as compared to the prior year driven by benefits from mix, pricing, price versus cost dynamics and productivity initiatives, partially offset by inflationary headwinds, acquisition integration costs and lower volumes across some of the Company's businesses.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing actions and growth in clean energy solutions and North American above-ground retail fueling equipment, partially offset by lower volumes in fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions due to temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed catalysts for growth, with new product launches and strategic dispositions contributing positively, while temporary macroeconomic factors negatively impact certain product lines.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing actions and growth in clean energy solutions and North American above-ground retail fueling equipment, partially offset by lower volumes in fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions due to temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand.

During the year ended December 31, 2024, the Company completed eight business acquisitions for approximately $674.0 million, net of cash acquired and inclusive of measurement period adjustments and contingent consideration. On March 31, 2024, the Company completed the sale of the De-Sta-Co business, an operating company within the Engineered Products segment, for total consideration, net of cash transferred of $675.9 million. This sale resulted in a pre-tax gain on disposition of $530.3 million.

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are healthy, supporting demand growth. The performance was driven by pricing actions, growth in clean energy solutions, and strategic initiatives, though there were temporary macro-driven market headwinds affecting certain segments.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing actions and growth in clean energy solutions and North American above-ground retail fueling equipment, partially offset by lower volumes in fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions due to temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand.
The results were driven by acquisitions, solid demand across most end markets and strategic pricing initiatives.

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are accelerating in some areas, particularly in clean energy and imaging segments, and contributing to demand with favorable trends in key end markets. However, there are temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand in certain segments, such as fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing actions and growth in clean energy solutions and North American above-ground retail fueling equipment, partially offset by lower volumes in fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions due to temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand.

The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by improved production performance and increased demand in our vehicle service business, along with favorable demand trends in our aerospace & defense business. We expect positive organic growth in 2025, driven by favorable demand trends in several of our key end markets, most notably in our aerospace and defense businesses.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, driven by reduced demand and higher carrying costs. There is a general reduction in customers' and distribution channels' inventory levels due to factors such as lead time normalization and increased interest rates.

evidence.text: The organic revenue decline was primarily due to reduced year-over-year demand in above ground retail fueling equipment, due to the expected roll-off of EMV-related demand in the first half of the year, as well as general reduction in our customers' inventory across our distribution channels, as higher interest rates increased its carrying costs.

The organic revenue decline was primarily due to general reduction in our customers' and distribution channels' inventory levels that resulted from lead time normalization and higher inventory carrying costs driven by interest rate increases.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety were stable, with no significant incidents or recalls reported across the referenced subsections.

evidence.text: Product quality and safety incidents are not mentioned as significant issues in the provided text.

Throughout this MD&A, we refer to measures used by management to evaluate performance, including a number of financial measures that are not defined under accounting principles generally accepted in the United States of America ('GAAP'). Please see 'Non-GAAP Disclosures' at the end of this Item 7 for further detail on these financial measures. Reconciliations within this MD&A provide more details on the use and derivation of these measures.

factors.summary: Aftermarket service revenue is growing, driven by factors such as increased demand, installed base expansion, pricing actions, and favorable demand trends across key end markets. However, there are temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting certain segments like fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions, which have resulted in lower volumes in these areas.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing actions and growth in clean energy solutions and North American above-ground retail fueling equipment, partially offset by lower volumes in fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions due to temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand.

The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by improved production performance and increased demand in our vehicle service business, along with favorable demand trends in our aerospace & defense business. We expect positive organic growth in 2025, driven by favorable demand trends in several of our key end markets, most notably in our aerospace and defense businesses.

factors.summary: R&D investment remains stable, with slight variations in annual figures, contributing to product innovation and growth.

evidence.text: The segment earnings increase was primarily driven by favorable product mix, pricing and productivity initiatives, partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation.

Research and development costs, including qualifying engineering costs, are expensed when incurred and amounted to $149.6 million, $139.1 million and $151.4 million for the years ended December 31, 2024, 2023 and 2022, respectively. These costs as a percent of revenue were 1.9%, 1.8% and 1.9% for the years December 31, 2024, 2023 and 2022, respectively.

factors.summary: New products have contributed positively to revenue growth, though there are some market headwinds affecting certain product lines.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was primarily driven by pricing actions and growth in clean energy solutions and North American above-ground retail fueling equipment, partially offset by lower volumes in fluid transfer solutions and vehicle wash solutions due to temporary macro-driven market headwinds impacting customer demand.
The results were driven by acquisitions, solid demand across most end markets and strategic pricing initiatives.

factors.summary: Digital technologies and automation are being adopted, enhancing operational efficiency and driving gains. While there is evidence of positive contributions, the impact of foreign currency translation presents some challenges.

evidence.text: Digital technologies and automation are enhancing operational efficiency and driving gains.
The segment earnings increase was primarily driven by favorable product mix, pricing and productivity initiatives, partially offset by an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation.
